task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jurism window is visible on the screen.
The 'Trash' item is highlighted or selected in the left pane of the Jurism window.

task_completion.description: The target cases are found in the Trash.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The cases 'Brown v. Board of Education', 'Miranda v. Arizona', and 'Gideon v. Wainwright' are visible in the central list view while the Trash folder is selected.

task_completion.description: The agent restores the cases from the Trash.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A context menu with 'Restore to Library' is open while the target cases are selected, or the cases are being dragged to 'My Library', or the cases disappear from the Trash view.

task_completion.description: The target cases are successfully restored to the library.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'My Library' folder is selected in the left pane, and the three target cases ('Brown v. Board of Education', 'Miranda v. Arizona', and 'Gideon v. Wainwright') are visible in the central list view.

integrity.description: The agent used the Jurism graphical interface to restore the cases.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show mouse clicks, selections, and context menus within the Jurism application, rather than terminal commands modifying the database.

integrity.description: The agent did not permanently delete the cases.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Delete Permanently' option is never clicked, and no warning dialogs about permanent deletion are confirmed.

integrity.description: The agent restored the existing items rather than creating new ones.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent does not use the 'New Item' button or manually type in the case names to create duplicate entries.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task involves restoring three specific U.S. Supreme Court cases in the Jurism application: 'Brown v. Board of Education', 'Miranda v. Arizona', and 'Gideon v. Wainwright'. These cases must be moved from the Trash back to 'My Library' without being permanently deleted.
